scope: Implement a minimal in-memory Todo REST API with 6 endpoints (GET/POST /todos, GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /todos/{id}) using in-memory storage only
acceptanceCriteria: GET /todos returns empty array on startup or list of all todos
POST /todos creates todo with title and optional description, returns 201 with created todo including generated id and completed=false
GET /todos/{id} returns todo or 404 if not found
PUT /todos/{id} fully replaces todo, returns 200 or 404
PATCH /todos/{id} partially updates todo fields, returns 200 or 404
DELETE /todos/{id} deletes todo and returns 204 or 404
Invalid input returns 400 with error message
All responses are JSON with consistent structure
qaChecks: Verify HTTP status codes match RESTful semantics (201, 200, 204, 400, 404)
Test idempotency of PUT and DELETE operations
Validate input rejection for missing required fields or invalid data types
Verify JSON response consistency across all endpoints
Test concurrent request handling for basic data integrity
Confirm in-memory storage is isolated (data resets on restart)
taskBreakdown: Initialize project structure and in-memory storage model
Implement GET/PUT/PATCH/DELETE /todos/{id} endpoints with validation and error handling
definitionOfDone: All 6 endpoints are functional with proper HTTP semantics, input validation returns appropriate errors, and all responses are consistent JSON format
